text:
• Good question-no, these are not WiredTiger tickets. They're a separate layer.
Three Different Queuing Systems
Layer
Mechanism
Network
Ingress rate limiter
Execution
Execution control queues
Storage
WiredTiger tickets
So the flow is:
Request arrives
[Ingress Rate Limiter] - mongotune can throttle here
[Execution Control Queue] - dwell time measured here (read/write)
[WiredTiger Tickets]
Storage
What it controls
New requests entering the server
Operations waiting to run
Concurrent access to storage engine
The dwell time metrics come from the execution control queues (serverStatus.queues.execution), which is a MongoDB server feature that limits concurrent operations before they hit WiredTiger.
The policy monitors dwell time at the execution control layer to detect when operations are backing up, then throttles at the ingress layer to stop more work from piling in.
